seoDescription: Every document and eligibility rule for Paraguay residency by investment — CIE, permanent residency and the cédula, explained route by route.

faqs.question: Do I need to speak Spanish to qualify for the Investor Pass?
faqs.answer: No. There is no language test at the residency stage. Language ability becomes relevant only later, if and when a resident applies for citizenship by naturalisation, which does carry civics and language expectations.

faqs.question: Do all my documents need to be apostilled, or just some?
faqs.answer: Any document issued outside Paraguay and used in the file — birth certificate, marriage certificate, criminal record — needs an apostille if the issuing country is a Hague Convention signatory, or consular legalisation if it is not. Documents obtained inside Paraguay during the process do not need an apostille.

faqs.question: Can I use a translation done in my home country?
faqs.answer: It is not reliably accepted. Paraguayan authorities generally require translation by a traductor público registered in Paraguay, so this step is normally completed in Asunción as part of file preparation, even if the underlying documents were apostilled abroad.

faqs.question: How long does the whole process take, from investment to cédula?
faqs.answer: The CIE is issued within five business days of a complete file. Permanent residency is a separate process, typically running several months. The cédula is then registered once residency is approved. Treat these as three sequential clocks, not one.

faqs.question: Does the Foreign Investor Certificate mean I already have residency?
faqs.answer: No. The CIE confirms the investment qualifies; it is issued by the MIC. Permanent residency is a separate status granted afterward by the Dirección Nacional de Migraciones. They are commonly confused, including by other guides — on this site they are always kept apart.

faqs.question: Can my spouse and children be included in the same application?
faqs.answer: Generally yes, with route-specific and case-specific detail confirmed before filing. Under the film route specifically, dependants typically receive two-year temporary residency initially rather than the direct permanent residency the main applicant receives, with a standard conversion pathway afterward — an example of why the fine print varies by route.

faqs.question: Which route has the simplest document file?
faqs.answer: The financial instruments route is the most administratively contained: no company to form, no property title to diligence, and no business plan — just the personal document set plus a single certificate from a Central Bank–authorised entity. Real estate and SUACE carry additional route-specific paperwork (title, or corporate formation documents) on top of the core file.

blocks.h1: Paraguay residency requirements, checklist by checklist.
blocks.lead: A qualifying investment from USD 40,000, a complete document file, and two sequential approvals — the Foreign Investor Certificate and permanent residency itself. Here is the full checklist, stage by stage, with the caveats most guides leave out.
blocks.bgAlt: Paraguay landscape at dusk — residency requirements checklist

blocks.eyebrow: The single most useful thing to know
blocks.heading: Three approvals, three requirement sets.

paragraphs.text: Guides that treat “Paraguay residency requirements” as a single checklist are doing applicants a disservice. There are three distinct credentials, issued by two different authorities, on two different timelines — and every document in this checklist exists to support one of them.
Citizenship is a fourth, later step — by naturalisation, after the constitutional residence period, not conferred by the investment. It has its own requirements and sits outside this checklist.

table.label: Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE)
table.value: Issued by the Ministry of Industry and Commerce (MIC), via SUACE. Confirms the investment qualifies — it is not residency. Typically 5 business days from a complete file.

table.label: Permanent residency
table.value: Granted by the Dirección Nacional de Migraciones. The actual legal-residency status, granted directly — no two-year temporary stage. Runs on a separate timeline, typically several months.

table.label: Cédula (national ID)
table.value: Issued by Policía Nacional / Identificaciones. The physical ID card that lets a resident open accounts, obtain a RUC and function locally. Registered after residency is approved.

asideNotice.title: Keeping the file from bouncing back
asideNotice.body: Every document below supports one of these three approvals. Keeping the separation in mind is what usually keeps a file from being returned for a missing annex.

cards.title: Age
cards.text: Applicants must be adults; dependent children are included on the main applicant's file, not assessed independently.

cards.title: Nationality
cards.text: Open to foreign nationals generally. A small number of visa-restricted nationalities require an additional letter from Paraguay's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MRE) before travel.

cards.title: Health
cards.text: A standard medical certificate is required; there is no health-based exclusion beyond that.

cards.title: Criminal record
cards.text: A clean criminal record from the country of origin, and from any country of recent residence, is required and assessed — not merely requested.

cards.title: Source of funds
cards.text: The qualifying investment must be traceable to a legitimate source. Expect to document the paper trail behind the capital, not just proof it exists today.

cards.title: Family inclusion
cards.text: Spouses and dependent children can generally be included in the same application; specifics depend on the route and are confirmed before anything is filed.

blocks.heading: The core document set.
blocks.lead: These documents apply no matter which of the five investment routes is chosen. Route-specific investment evidence is covered separately below.

cards.title: Identity & travel
cards.text:
Valid passport with at least six months' remaining validity and legible copies of the biographical page.
Passport-sized photographs to Migraciones' format.

cards.title: Civil status
cards.text:
Birth certificate, apostilled and translated.
Marriage certificate, if a spouse is included.
Birth certificates for any dependent children.

cards.title: Background
cards.text:
Criminal record certificate from the country of origin, apostilled and dated recently.
Criminal record certificate from any country of recent residence.
MRE letter, for the small number of visa-restricted nationalities.

cards.title: Health
cards.text: A medical certificate confirming general fitness, issued to the standard the file requires.

cards.title: Investment evidence
cards.text: Documentation proving the qualifying investment — the specific form varies by route — plus, where applicable, evidence of the source of the invested funds.

cards.title: Administrative
cards.text:
Completed migration application forms.
Power of attorney, where the file is progressed by counsel on the applicant's behalf.

blocks.eyebrow: The two rules that trip people up
blocks.heading: Apostille and translation.

paragraphs.text: Apostille. Any document issued outside Paraguay and destined for a Paraguayan file — birth certificate, marriage certificate, criminal record — must carry an apostille under the Hague Apostille Convention if the issuing country is a signatory. Non-signatory countries require consular legalisation instead, which takes longer and should be started early. Apostilles can only be applied to originals, not photocopies.

Translation. Once apostilled, foreign-language documents need a certified Spanish translation. Only a traductor público — a sworn translator registered in Paraguay — is reliably accepted; translations completed abroad are frequently rejected on review. In practice, translation is done on the ground in Asunción, as part of file preparation.

notice.title: How recent is “recent”
notice.body: Criminal record certificates and, for the real estate and financial-instruments routes, the investment-evidencing documents are generally expected to be dated within roughly 180 days of filing. Because apostille and translation can themselves take several weeks, order background documents early — but not so early that they age out before the file is submitted.

asideTable.label: Apostille
asideTable.value: Required for foreign-issued documents from Hague signatories; consular legalisation otherwise

asideTable.label: Translation
asideTable.value: Certified Spanish translation by a Paraguay-registered traductor público, done in Asunción

asideTable.label: Recency window
asideTable.value: ~180 days for criminal records & route-specific investment evidence

blocks.imageAlt: Legal document preparation for a Paraguay residency file

blocks.heading: Investment evidence, by route.

paragraphs.text: The personal document set above is constant across every route. What changes is the evidence of the qualifying investment itself.

table.label: Real estate — USD 200,000
table.value: A registered transfer deed, or a notarised purchase contract with at least 30% of the declared value paid, for qualifying income-generating property. See the Paraguay real estate residency route.

table.label: Financial instruments — USD 200,000
table.value: A certificate from a Central Bank–authorised entity confirming the value and the required two-year holding term. See Paraguay financial instruments residency.

table.label: Approved tourism projects — USD 150,000
table.value: Evidence of the investment into a MIC-approved tourism development, with the project's business plan and reporting schedule. See Paraguay tourism investment residency.

table.label: SUACE company — USD 70,000
table.value: Evidence the company has been formed and capitalised to threshold through the SUACE system, with supporting corporate documentation. See the SUACE Paraguay company residency route.

table.label: Film & audiovisual — USD 40,000
table.value: Evidence of the contribution to an approved production, plus a separate demonstration of at least USD 80,000 in available funds. See the Paraguay film residency route.

notice.title: One unconfirmed detail
notice.body: Third-party sources suggest the SUACE productive-investment track may also expect a business plan and possibly a job-creation requirement tied to the capitalised company. That detail is not yet reflected in the published resolution text we have reviewed — treat it as indicative, and confirm the current position with counsel before structuring the company.

asideNotice.title: The film route sits apart
asideNotice.body: Film & audiovisual investment runs through a separate, pre-existing pathway administered via Paraguay's audiovisual institute (INAP) under Ley N° 6106/2018 — not Resolution N° 0283/2026. It reaches the same residency and citizenship destination, but its documentation and timeline are governed by different rules. See the Paraguay film residency route for the honest caveats.

blocks.imageAlt: Modern residential apartment tower in Asunción, Paraguay

blocks.heading: The sequence, at a high level.

paragraphs.text: Because Investor Pass applicants bypass the standard two-year temporary-residency stage, the overall file looks different from Paraguay's ordinary immigration process — shorter in structure, but not instant.

notice.title: One honest caveat
notice.body: Anyone quoting a single headline timeline for “getting residency” is usually citing the five-day CIE figure alone. Permanent residency runs on its own, longer clock.

items.title: Route selection & strategy
items.text: Confirm which of the five routes fits your objectives and budget — see the full route comparison for how they differ in capital, control and ongoing obligations.

items.title: Document collection
items.text: Apostille and translate the civil-status and background documents in parallel with structuring the investment — usually the longest single stretch of the process.

items.title: The qualifying investment
items.text: The investment is made and documented to the standard the route requires — purchase, capitalisation, deployment, or contribution.

items.title: Foreign Investor Certificate
items.text: With a complete file, the MIC issues the CIE within five business days — the fastest, most predictable step in the process.

items.title: Permanent residency
items.text: The file is progressed with the Dirección Nacional de Migraciones, typically over several months, on a separate clock from the CIE.

items.title: The cédula
items.text: Once residency is approved, the applicant registers for the Paraguayan national ID card — unlocking bank accounts, a RUC and daily proof of identity.

items.title: Maintaining status
items.text: Permanent residency is maintained with at least one visit to Paraguay every three years — a low bar by regional standards, but a real one.

items.title: Citizenship, later
items.text: After three years of permanent residence, and subject to language, civics and physical-presence requirements, a resident may apply for citizenship by naturalisation — a separate application, not an automatic outcome.

blocks.heading: What tends to cause delay.
blocks.lead: The documents above are all obtainable by any applicant; what causes real delay is almost always sequencing and detail, not eligibility.

cards.title: Documents aging out
cards.text: Apostilled criminal records and investment-evidencing certificates have a practical shelf life. Ordering them too early means re-doing them.

cards.title: Translation done in the wrong place
cards.text: A translation completed abroad, however well-intentioned, is frequently not accepted. Budget for translation in Asunción, as part of the process.

cards.title: Undisclosed criminal history
cards.text: Even minor, resolved matters should be disclosed and explained rather than omitted — omissions surface in checks and cost far more time.

cards.title: Source-of-funds gaps
cards.text: An investment that is real but poorly documented at origin slows both the bank side and the residency file.

cards.title: Treating the CIE as the finish line
cards.text: The certificate is a real milestone, but it is not residency. Plan around the several-months residency figure, not the five-day CIE figure.

blocks.pull: A complete file, prepared properly the first time.
blocks.lead: Request guidance on the documents your route requires and the realistic timeline for your circumstances.
blocks.bgAlt: Asunción waterfront skyline at dusk, Paraguay
